hypothesis: Incorporating a learnable spectral subtraction module between the encoder and decoder—trained to iteratively remove the reconstructed pure-component spectrum of one identified constituent from the mixture representation before predicting the next—will enable the model to identify all components of a mixture simultaneously without requiring molecular formula prompts, thereby achieving higher Top-10 accuracy on ternary and higher-order mixtures than the current prompt-conditioned architecture while remaining testable by comparing component identification accuracy against the existing formula-conditioned baseline on held-out ternary NIST mixtures.
